input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
Our `textd` service still falls over during a long refresh run in the constrained CI profile. Short checks on a fresh start look normal, but the full validation does not survive repeated processor swaps.

Get the daemon through the long run without changing the deterministic text-processing results from the shipped revisions.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#

New Terminal Output:
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ls -R
.:
bin  build  plugin-src  src

./bin:
build  integration  run-suite  stress

./build:
main-classes  main-sources.txt  plugin-classes  plugins

./build/main-classes:

./build/plugin-classes:

./build/plugins:

./plugin-src:
io

./plugin-src/io:
harbor

./plugin-src/io/harbor:
textd

./plugin-src/io/harbor/textd:
customer

./plugin-src/io/harbor/textd/customer:
CustomerPluginFactory.java  CustomerPluginModule.java  PipelineProcessor.java  PipelineStages.java

./src:
main

./src/main:
java

./src/main/java:
io

./src/main/java/io:
harbor

./src/main/java/io/harbor:
textd

./src/main/java/io/harbor/textd:
api  cli  core

./src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/api:
PluginModule.java  PluginModuleFactory.java  TextProcessor.java

./src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/cli:
HotReloadIntegrationRunner.java  HotReloadStressRunner.java  RuntimeDigestRunner.java

./src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/core:
LoadedGeneration.java  ProcessingResult.java  ThreadBridge.java  TimelineFrame.java   WorkerHandle.java
OutputFormat.java      TextDaemon.java        ThreadCache.java   TimelineLedger.java
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# cat bin/stress
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)

if [[ "${TEXTD_SKIP_BUILD:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  "$ROOT_DIR/bin/build"
fi

java -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=64m -cp "$ROOT_DIR/build/main-classes" \
  io.harbor.textd.cli.HotReloadStressRunner \
  "$ROOT_DIR/build/plugins/customer-default.jar" \
  "${1:-500}"
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/stress

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/stress
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: Could not initialize class io.harbor.textd.customer.PipelineStages
        at io.harbor.textd.customer.PipelineProcessor.process(PipelineProcessor.java:22)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.WorkerHandle.process(WorkerHandle.java:21)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.TextDaemon.processRecord(TextDaemon.java:80)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.TextDaemon.lambda$processBatch$0(TextDaemon.java:43)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:264)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1136)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:635)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
Caused by: java.lang.ExceptionInInitializerError: Exception java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Metaspace [in thread "textd-worker-1"]
        at io.harbor.textd.customer.PipelineStages.<clinit>(PipelineStages.java:10)
        at io.harbor.textd.customer.PipelineProcessor.process(PipelineProcessor.java:22)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.WorkerHandle.process(WorkerHandle.java:21)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.TextDaemon.processRecord(TextDaemon.java:80)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.TextDaemon.lambda$processBatch$0(TextDaemon.java:43)
        at io.harbor.textd.core.TextDaemon$$Lambda$1/0x00007dcf51001c58.call(Unknown Source)
        ... 4 more
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/stress

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/stress
stress-ok cycles=500
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#

{
  "analysis": "The resource management logic seems robust if `unpin()` is called correctly. 
- `TimelineFrame` starts with `pins = 1`.
- `TimelineLedger.install` creates a new frame and calls `previous.cool()`.
- `cool()` calls `unpin()`, decrementing `pins` from 1 to 0 (assuming no other pins).
- If `pins` reaches 0 and `cooled` is true, it calls `closeLoadedGeneration()`, which closes the `URLClassLoader`.

In the buggy version, `WorkerHandle` was pinning the frame but never unpinning it because `close()` wasn't called when the cursor was swapped. This kept `pins > 0`, so the `ClassLoader` was never closed.

With my fix in `ThreadCache.java`, `cursor.close()` is called, which calls `frame.unpin()`. This allows the pin count to reach 0 when the generation is no longer the current one and no longer active in any worker thread's cache.

I should run the integration suite as well to make sure I haven't broken anything.",
  "plan": "1. Run `./bin/run-suite` to ensure all tests pass.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "./bin/run-suite
",
      "duration": 5.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/run-suite

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/run-suite

Previous response had warnings:
WARNINGS: - Extra text detected after JSON object

New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd# ./bin/run-suite
suite-ok
root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#

Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:

root@6d4cae255124:/workspace/textd#


Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.
